commit.message:
feat: rework ticket search, fix Jira worklogs, add Clockify projects

- Rework description input: clicking shows assigned tickets dropdown,
  typing queries Jira, selecting auto-fills description with summary
  and shows ticket key badge. Removed separate toggle/count panel.
- Fix Jira time logging: worklog comment now uses ADF (Atlassian
  Document Format) required by v3 API instead of plain string.
- Add Clockify project selection: new GetProjects() API, populate
  project dropdown, pass projectId to StartTimer/CreateTimeEntry.
- Update all tests, mock server, and Wails bindings.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix CLI NuGet packaging: exclude .playwright/ but keep Playwright runtime support (#25)

* Exclude Playwright files from CLI tool NuGet package

Playwright's browser/node infrastructure was being bundled into the
CLI tool package, bloating it from ~2.7MB to ~47MB. This also caused
DotnetToolSettings.xml packaging failures in versions 0.4.0 and 0.5.0,
making it impossible to install or update the CLI tool past 0.3.0.

Exclude .playwright files from the pack output via MSBuild targets.
Playwright browsers are installed separately at runtime via the
playwright.ps1 script when Mermaid rendering is needed.

Fixes #21

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refine Playwright exclusion to keep playwright.ps1 bootstrap script

The previous fix excluded all Playwright files including playwright.ps1,
which users need to install Chromium browsers for Mermaid diagram
rendering. This revision narrows the exclusion to only the .playwright/
directory (the Node.js package tree with ~325 files), while preserving:

- Microsoft.Playwright.dll (C# API for Mermaid rendering)
- playwright.ps1 (bootstrap script for browser installation)

Package remains at ~2.7MB with DotnetToolSettings.xml intact.

Fixes #21

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Mermaid tests and fix environment-dependent test failures

- Fix MultipleDiagramsInOneDocument to verify document structure instead
  of a hardcoded byte threshold that fails without Playwright browsers
- Tag Playwright-dependent tests with [Trait("Category", "Playwright")]
  so CI can filter them when browser deps aren't available
- Add 8 new Mermaid tests covering:
  - Disabled rendering preserves source code in output
  - Fallback contains original Mermaid source
  - Surrounding text preserved around diagrams
  - Enabled vs disabled produces different output
  - Whitespace-only blocks trigger empty fallback
  - Default options have Mermaid enabled
  - Non-mermaid languages are not treated as Mermaid
  - Custom dimension constraints

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Darrel Miller (from Dev Box) <darrmi@microsoft.com>

fix: run coverage only on ubuntu to avoid PowerShell flag parsing issue

On Windows, PowerShell splits '-coverprofile=coverage.out' into two
tokens at the '.' — treating '.out' as a package path. Since coverage
is only uploaded from ubuntu-latest anyway, simply gate both the
coverage run and upload steps with 'if: matrix.os == ubuntu-latest'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[Security] Fix code injection vulnerability in Formstack autopopulation (#4364)

* [Security] Fix code injection in Formstack autopopulation (Closes #4363)

- Replace unsafe eval() with Django template Variable system
- Add resolve_field_expression() function for safe expression resolution
- Only allow safe attribute access (user.first_name, user.email, etc)
- Prevent arbitrary code execution while maintaining functionality

Reported by Varun Yadav (Security Researcher)

* [Accessibility]: Add missing alt text to images in key user-facing templates. Fixes #4425

* Revert "[Accessibility]: Add missing alt text to images in key user-facing templates. Fixes #4425"

This reverts commit 2f04c42ad97677d8d7a532ac6359c4b1458c5fac.

* Remove unrelated django_settings.py change from accessibility PR

* Fix EMAIL_PORT type (int) and remove duplicate email settings

* Add tests for FormstackAppModule._resolve_field_expression

* Fix resolve_field_expression: broad exception handling and if value is not None

* Minor cleanup in formstackappmodule

* Restore _resolve_field_expression for test compatibility (safe implementation)

* Fix lint errors in formstackappmodule

- Fix import ordering (move imports to top, after logging setup)
- Fix line length violations (split long lines)
- Fix spacing around operators and keywords
- Fix inline comment spacing
- Add proper blank lines between functions

* Remove redundant _resolve_field_expression and test the production function

Tests were calling self.module._resolve_field_expression() (manual getattr
traversal) but production code calls resolve_field_expression() (Django
Variable/Context resolver). These have different implementations.

Fixed by:
- Deleting unused _resolve_field_expression instance method
- Updating tests to import and call resolve_field_expression directly
- Removing unnecessary setUp that instantiated the module

Now tests validate the actual production code path.

* Add explicit guard for empty/whitespace expressions

- Add early return for empty or whitespace-only expressions before
  calling Variable() to avoid ValueError
- Add test_resolve_whitespace_expression to verify whitespace-only
  input returns None
- Aligns with studentapp loop which already strips lines

This prevents logging unnecessary exceptions for malformed input.

* Use try/finally in test_resolve_empty_string_value

Ensures user.username is always restored even if assertion fails,
preventing test pollution that could affect subsequent tests.

* Fix IntegrityError when accessing admin/program/studentapplication/add/ (#4554)

- Make program and user fields nullable to allow Django Admin form instantiation
- Add guard to set_questions() to prevent crashes on unsaved instances
- Add post_save signal handler with recursive prevention for M2M operations
- Fix mutable default argument in get_forms() to prevent cross-request data bleed
- Add date field guard in BaseAppElement.update() for compatibility
- Use django.utils.timezone.now for timezone-aware timestamps

Fixes #4554

* Address Copilot review: handle None values, validate field names, strip expressions, add tests

* Revert "Fix IntegrityError when accessing admin/program/studentapplication/add/ (#4554)"

This reverts commit b4b43d19a87f23e01c1fe1fcfd8dd45adf6da99a.

* Add docstrings and clean up resolve_field_expression and tests

* Add test for leading whitespace in expressions from colon-separated config

* Refactor: strip expression early and simplify guard clause

* Consolidate guard clause in resolve_field_expression

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Align program modules tests init with main import header

* Restore settings

* Address review cleanup suggestions

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: William Gearty <willgearty@gmail.com>

feat: first-class OpenTelemetry and resiliency framework

New crates:

oxide-telemetry — OpenTelemetry-compatible tracing for WASM apps:
  - Automatic instrumentation via runtime hooks (signal lifecycle,
    effect execution, batch operations)
  - W3C trace context propagation (traceparent header on fetch)
  - traced_fetch() for distributed tracing across frontend/backend
  - Span model with attributes, duration, trace/span IDs
  - In-memory span buffer with stats (signals created/read/written,
    effects run, total effect time)
  - RAII SpanGuard for manual instrumentation
  - Zero overhead when not initialized (Cell-based fn pointer check)

oxide-resiliency — Production resiliency patterns:
  - error_boundary() — catch panics, render fallback UI
  - default_error_boundary() — styled error card
  - retry() — async retry with exponential/fixed backoff
  - CircuitBreaker — failure threshold + auto-recovery
    (Closed → Open → HalfOpen state machine via signals)
  - with_timeout() — async operation timeout
  - sleep() — WASM-compatible async sleep

Runtime hooks system (oxide-core):
  - HookEvent enum: SignalCreate, SignalRead, SignalWrite,
    EffectRun, EffectComplete, BatchStart, BatchEnd
  - set_hook(fn) / clear_hook() — zero-cost when unused
  - High-resolution timestamps via js_sys::Date::now()

Showcase: 18 interactive demos (was 16), 219 KB WASM bundle

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add interactive lightbox for gallery images

Features:
- Click any gallery thumbnail to open full-size preview modal
- Close button (X) in top right corner
- Click outside the image to close
- Press Escape key to close
- Dark overlay for focus on image
- Smooth fade-in animation
- Prevents body scrolling when modal is open

CSS additions:
- .image-modal: Fixed position fullscreen overlay
- .modal-content: Responsive image container
- .modal-close: Styled close button with hover effect
- .gallery-item: Added cursor pointer for clickability

JS additions:
- Event listeners for gallery items
- Modal open/close handlers
- Multiple close methods (button, outside click, escape key)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix: Restore WithExternalHttpEndpoints() for Vite app resource

The .WithExternalHttpEndpoints() call was incorrectly removed from the
Vite app builder chain in PR #29. This method is essential for making
the frontend externally accessible from the Aspire orchestrator.

Without this method, Aspire creates an internal resource that is not
exposed to the network, preventing the Vite dev server from being
accessible to external clients (including E2E tests).

Fix restores the method call to allow the dev server to accept external
connections on its dynamically allocated port.

Fixes #33

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Distributed worker sandbox model and monitor-only UI

- Refactor distributed worker to use in-memory SQLite sandboxes per job
  instead of thread-local persistent connections (_open_job_sandbox)
- Extract distributed_payloads.py for job context seeding and result
  payload extraction
- Extract write_local_ai_split_tables as a standalone function from
  ModuleRunner for reuse by the distributed worker
- Add monitor-only mode to the Electron frontend so the UI can track
  distributed worker progress without controlling it
- Disable control buttons in ProgressDashboard during monitor-only mode
- Add copy-to-clipboard buttons for coordinator URL and worker command
  in SettingsView
- Forward force flag to coordinator job claims
- Update distributed worker tests for the sandbox approach

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Pipeline] fix: restore test-contract UI text and CSS class names (#413)

Closes #411

## Summary

This PR fixes 6 failing CI tests on `main` that resulted from UI text
and CSS class name mismatches between the implementation and the tests.

## PRD Fidelity

No PRD drift — this is a regression fix aligned with existing test
contracts.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Pipeline.cshtml` | Revert `.event-log` height to `24rem`; add
`text-sm` class to `#eventLog` div |
| `_Layout.cshtml` | Add `status-bar-brand-row` to brand div,
`status-bar-links` to nav links div |
| `site.css` | Add `.status-bar-links` flex rules (`flex-wrap`, `flex: 0
0 auto`, `white-space: nowrap`); alias `.evidence-val` = `.metric-val` |
| `Index.cshtml` | Use `evidence-val` class for pipeline runs stat |
| `Operator.cshtml` | Align section headings to test-specified text; add
`FailureSignature` code-pill; rename "evidence trail" → "decision trail"
|

## Tests Fixed

- `PipelinePageTests.PipelinePage_KeepsReplayEventLogHeightStable` —
height 24rem ✅
-
`NavigationLayoutTests.SharedNavigation_RendersDedicatedLinkRowWithDistinctTargets`
— status-bar-brand-row ✅
-
`NavigationLayoutTests.SharedNavigation_CssPreventsNavLinksFromShrinkingIntoNeighborTargets`
— .status-bar-links CSS ✅
- `RunHistoryTests.EvidenceStrip_ShowsZeroWhenNoRuns` — evidence-val
class ✅
- `OperatorPageTests.Operator_RendersBlockedQueuedAndAutonomousSections`
— heading text ✅
- `OperatorPageTests.Operator_RendersPastRunsSection` — Past Runs
heading + failure signature ✅

## Test Results

```
Test Run Successful.
Passed: 165
```

commit.message:
Optimize streaming performance: StringBuilder, cached VM refs, skip height estimation

Three concrete performance improvements for streaming delta processing:

1. StringBuilder for content accumulation (ChatViewModel.cs)
   - Before: accContent += delta (O(n²) — each += copies entire string)
   - After: accContent.Append(delta) (O(n) amortized)
   - Applied to both content and reasoning accumulators

2. Cached ViewModel references (ChatViewModel.cs)
   - Before: Messages.LastOrDefault(m => m.Message == streamingMsg) on every delta
   - After: streamingVm / reasoningVm captured when message is first created
   - Eliminates O(n) linear scan per delta event

3. Skip height estimation during streaming (TranscriptItems.cs)
   - Before: EstimateWrappedTextHeight called on every content change (does
     Replace + Split on full text — O(n) per call, O(n²) total)
   - After: Skipped during streaming, recalculated once when streaming ends
   - Applied to both AssistantMessageItem and ReasoningItem

All state cleanup paths (error, abort, message complete, reasoning complete)
properly reset the new variables.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Exclude Playwright files from CLI tool NuGet package (#24)

Playwright's browser/node infrastructure was being bundled into the
CLI tool package, bloating it from ~2.7MB to ~47MB. This also caused
DotnetToolSettings.xml packaging failures in versions 0.4.0 and 0.5.0,
making it impossible to install or update the CLI tool past 0.3.0.

Exclude .playwright files from the pack output via MSBuild targets.
Playwright browsers are installed separately at runtime via the
playwright.ps1 script when Mermaid rendering is needed.

Fixes #21

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix soft line breaks being dropped in Word output (#23)

Consecutive markdown lines within the same paragraph (e.g. metadata
fields separated by newlines but no blank line) were merging into a
single line in Word because soft line breaks were silently ignored.

Now both hard and soft line breaks produce a Break element in the
Word paragraph, preserving the line separation the author intended.

Fixes #22

commit.message:
fix(resize): activity-aware PTY sizing, better replay, mobile persistence (#124)

When resuming a session from a laptop after using it on a phone, the PTY
stayed at the phone's small dimensions because recalcPtySize took the
minimum across all connected clients — including idle ones. This also
adds WebSocket keepalive pings, increases the scrollback buffer from
200KB to 1MB, and improves mobile background tab persistence with
instant reconnect on visibility change and Wake Lock API support.

Closes #120

---------

commit.message:
feat: implement MIT-SHM X11 extension for zero-copy image transfers

Add MIT-SHM (X Shared Memory Extension) support to optimize X11 rendering
performance by using shared memory for pixel data transfer instead of the
slower PutImage fallback.

Changes:
- Add internal/x11/shm package with MIT-SHM protocol implementation
  - ShmQueryVersion, ShmAttach, ShmDetach, ShmPutImage requests
  - Shared memory lifecycle management (shmget, shmat, shmdt, shmctl)
  - Extension query and version negotiation

- Extend X11 client with extension support
  - Add SendRequestAndReply method for request-reply pattern
  - Add ExtensionOpcode query method (QueryExtension request)
  - Support for dynamic extension base opcode lookup

- Add QueryExtension opcode (98) to wire protocol constants

- Update README to reflect MIT-SHM implementation
  - X11 client now 5 packages (~1,400 LOC)
  - Remove MIT-SHM from missing optimizations list
  - Update Phase 1 completion to 90%
  - Update priority contributions

- Mark PLAN.md steps 4, 5, and 10 as complete

Benefits:
- Zero-copy image transfers for X11 (shared memory vs socket copy)
- Significant performance improvement for frequent buffer updates
- Automatic fallback to PutImage when SHM unavailable
- Thread-safe extension detection and segment management

All tests pass (21 packages). No regressions in complexity metrics.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

scripts: Audit and clean up - remove stale scripts, fix outdated references

Removed scripts:
- test-wallet.sh, test-wallet.bat: No separate wallet binary; wallet
  functions are handled by time-cli (getbalance, listunspent, etc.)
- diagnose_fork_state.sh: SSH-based script hardcoded to old server
  names; referenced non-existent FORK_RESOLUTION_FIXES.md; fork
  resolution is now automatic (longest-chain)
- emergency_recovery.sh: SSH-based to hardcoded old infrastructure;
  referenced non-existent docs; no longer needed

Fixed scripts:
- diagnose_forks.sh: masternode-status -> masternodestatus; replaced
  references to non-existent FORK_ANALYSIS.md and
  FORK_RESOLUTION_DEPLOYMENT.md with docs/ references
- diagnose_status.sh: Added error handling to masternodestatus jq pipe
- check_node_state.sh: Removed stale 'deploy commit 5b876f0' message;
  replaced with actionable restart/reindex instructions
- test_critical_flow.sh: Removed BUG #1/#2/#3/#4 labels (all fixed);
  updated minimum version check from v1.1.0 to v1.2.0

Updated README.md:
- Removed deleted scripts from documentation
- Fixed wrong CLI commands: wallet create/balance -> getbalance/
  getwalletinfo; node info -> getblockcount
- Fixed wrong data path in troubleshooting (/etc/timecoin -> ~/.timecoin)
- Fixed wrong disk usage path (/var/lib/timecoin -> ~/.timecoin)
- Removed 'NEW!' badge from configure-masternode.sh entry
- Added documentation for testing and diagnostic scripts that were
  previously undocumented: test.sh, test_transaction.sh, test_timevote.sh,
  test_critical_flow.sh, test_finalization_propagation.sh,
  stability_test.sh, setup_local_testnet.sh, diagnose_forks.sh,
  check_node_state.sh, diagnose_status.sh, quick_check.sh

commit.message:
fix(concurrency): thread-safe fixes for DbContext, WebSocket

- DbContextWriteLock: fix TOCTOU race in Dispose() using Interlocked.CompareExchange
  instead of volatile bool (two threads could double-dispose SemaphoreSlim)
- CapitalComWebSocketService: fix CRITICAL deadlock in reconnect path where
  ConnectAsync holds _connectionLock then ResubscribeToAllEpicsAsync calls
  SubscribeToMarketInternalAsync which re-acquires the same non-reentrant
  SemaphoreSlim. Extracted SendSubscriptionMessageAsync (lock-free) for
  internal use when lock is already held.
- CapitalComWebSocketService: wrap all _connectionLock.Release() in
  SafeReleaseConnectionLock() to prevent ObjectDisposedException when
  Dispose() runs concurrently with a locked operation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
